Annotation

Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us
The patient shows elevated creatinine, proteinuria, glucosuria, ketonuria, and bilirubinuria, with low urine specific gravity and mildly decreased CO₂, suggesting impaired renal function (possible chronic kidney disease or acute kidney injury) and possible tubular dysfunction. Urine findings (cloudy, brown, ammoniacal) may indicate underlying urinary tract pathology. Low HbA1c with normoglycemia suggests recent changes in glucose metabolism. Recommend further nephrological evaluation and assessment for possible urinary tract or systemic disease.
